message:
feat(caut): caut v1 envelope schema support + provider arg migration [wa-3kxe]

Adapt caut integration to the v1 output schema and the --provider CLI
flag (replaces the deprecated --service flag):

API surface changes:
- Add CautService::provider_arg() returning the caut provider slug
  ("codex" for OpenAI)
- Add CautService::from_cli_input() for user-facing service parsing
- Add CautService::supported_cli_inputs() for CLI hint rendering
- Add "codex" as recognized OpenAI slug in is_openai_slug()
- Migrate build_args() from --service openai to --provider codex
- CautClient::refresh() now calls "usage" (caut removed "refresh"
  subcommand; provider-scoped usage does refresh+read in one step)

v1 envelope parsing:
- Add CautV1Envelope, CautV1UsageEntry, CautV1UsagePayload,
  CautV1Identity deserialization structs with flatten/default handling
- parse_usage_json() and parse_refresh_json() try direct parse first,
  then fall back to v1 envelope conversion
- Field extraction helpers (extract_usage_f64/u64/string) search
  multiple key aliases across primary/secondary usage payloads for
  forward compatibility (e.g., percentRemaining, percent_remaining,
  remainingPercent, quotaPercentRemaining)
- Value coercion helpers handle numeric-as-string and cross-type casts

Tests:
- parse_usage_supports_caut_v1_schema: full round-trip of v1 envelope
  with identity, usage metrics, and metadata
- parse_refresh_supports_caut_v1_schema: v1 refresh-via-usage parsing
- caut_service_from_cli_input_aliases: verifies openai/codex/chat-gpt
  mappings and anthropic rejection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
